vision.statement: Reduce migration risk by turning the program lead vision into reviewed epics, approved features, and sprint-ready stories.
vision.objective: Break the vision into epics, review each gate, and only create the next level of backlog items after approval.

epics.title: Planning workflow
epics.summary: Capture the vision and move it through review gates before new work is broken down.

epics.title: Confluence-grounded onboarding
epics.summary: Surface the onboarding agent and document-first guidance inside the workspace.

features.title: Review gate orchestration
features.summary: Gate the movement from vision to epics, then features, then stories.

features.title: Story readiness checks
features.summary: Hold stories until the Definition of Ready is satisfied.

features.title: Onboarding question flow
features.summary: Guide new joiners through role, tenure, and document-backed answers.

refinement.problemStatement: The team is working from messy notes, so the backlog is hard to read, hard to size, and difficult to commit to in sprint planning.
refinement.storySummary: As a refinement facilitator, I want to convert messy notes into a structured, sprint-ready story so that the team can review the work quickly and commit with confidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Given refinement notes, when the facilitator normalizes the content, then the output keeps the exact problem statement, story summary, acceptance criteria, dependencies, references, estimation, DoR validation, gaps, and normalization notes order.
Should flag any misalignment between the problem statement and the proposed story before the story is presented for sprint commitment.
Should preserve tool names, environments, and API references when they are mentioned in the notes.
dependencies: Program lead vision or backlog notes
references: Refinement notes from the backlog session
definitionOfReadyValidation: Problem statement present and aligned with the story
User story follows As a / I want / So that format
Acceptance criteria are present and testable
gaps: None identified during refinement — verify before sprint commitment
normalizationNotes: Reordered the output to match the story template exactly.
Preserved technical specificity from the notes.
Called out readiness gaps separately from the story body.

gaps: No performance/load scenarios were discussed during refinement.

testSuites.name: Refinement story normalization
testSuites.acceptanceCriterion: Given refinement notes, when the facilitator normalizes the content, then the output keeps the exact story structure.

preconditions: A messy refinement session summary exists
testCases.expectedResult: The structured story uses the exact output order and preserves the original context.
testCases.testData: Backlog notes with a problem statement, story summary, and acceptance criteria

preconditions: A story with mismatched problem and summary text exists
testCases.expectedResult: The UI surfaces an explicit clarification warning about the mismatch.
testCases.testData: Problem X with a story summary about Y

preconditions: The notes contain only partial context
testCases.expectedResult: Missing items are called out as DoR gaps instead of being invented.
testCases.testData: Notes with only dependencies and a sprint target

testSuites.name: QE test case generation
testSuites.acceptanceCriterion: Given acceptance criteria, when the generator produces test cases, then each criterion has positive, negative, and edge coverage.

preconditions: A story with clear acceptance criteria exists
testCases.expectedResult: At least one happy-path test case is generated for each criterion.
testCases.testData: Three acceptance criteria from the refinement story

testCases.expectedResult: The output flags the criterion as untestable and suggests a rewrite.

preconditions: Boundary values and empty inputs are mentioned
testCases.expectedResult: Boundary and edge cases are present and the test IDs remain sequential.

firstInteraction: Is this your first week, or have you been here a bit and have specific questions?

answers.question: How do I set up my dev environment?
answers.answer: Install the repo prerequisites, request the listed access, and follow the team onboarding notes before your first contribution.
nextQuestions: Want me to walk you through the full setup steps?
answers.suggestedEscalation: Ask the team lead if repo access is missing.

answers.question: What is our branching strategy?
answers.answer: Use the shared feature branch flow, keep pull requests small, and ask for review before merging.
warnings: Branching and release notes may be outdated - verify with your team lead.
nextQuestions: Want me to point you to the review checklist?
answers.suggestedEscalation: Ask the scrum master if the team needs a release-specific branching exception.

onboarding.fallback: I couldn't find this in our documentation. You may want to ask your team lead or #team-platform.
